title: Pyrethrum fuscatum
taxonomy: Pyrethrum fuscatum is classified as a species of flowering plant within the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pyrethrum, which is a group of flowering plants native to Europe and Asia. The species is further classified as a perennial daisy, with a range of subspecies and varieties found in different regions. Pyrethrum fuscatum is often found growing in moist, grassy areas and is known for its bright yellow flowers. This classification helps to identify and distinguish it from other related species within the Pyrethrum genus.

constituents: Pyrethrum fuscatum contains several active constituents, including pyrethrins and flavonoids. The pyrethrins, specifically pyrethrin I and pyrethrin II, are responsible for the plant's insecticidal properties. These compounds are known to disrupt the nervous system of insects, ultimately leading to their death. The flavonoids present in the plant have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. These active constituents contribute to the plant's potential use in natural pest control and medicinal applications.

intro: Pyrethrum fuscatum, also known as the dark pyrethrum or pyrethrum daisy, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating a range of conditions, from wounds and skin irritations to respiratory issues and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Pyrethrum fuscatum include pyrethrins, flavonoids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pyrethrum fuscatum include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
